title: Tests should include assertions
description: The rule targets test methods that lack an assertion and consist solely of an action and, optionally, a setup. public void Add_SingleNumber_ReturnsSameNumber() var stringCalculator = new StringCalculator(); var actual = stringCalculator.Add("0"); Such tests only verify that the system under test does not throw any exceptions without providing any guarantees regarding the code’s behavior under test. Those tests increase the coverage without enforcing anything on the covered code, resulting in a false sense of security.
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

checks.message: Test methods should include assertions to verify expected behavior

logic.reasoning: Detects test methods (marked with Test/TestMethod/Fact/Theory attributes) that don't contain any assertion statements.
